ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the country is located */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the country (e.g., high, upper-middle, lower-middle, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the population group (e.g., children, adults, elderly) */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range for the population group (e.g., 0-14, 15-64, 65+) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the population group (e.g., male, female, both) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "food_groups" (
  "food_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each food group */,
  "food_name" TEXT /* Name of the food group (e.g., fruits, vegetables, meat products) */,
  "food_category" TEXT /* Category of the food group (e.g., plant-based, animal-based) */,
  "sub_category" TEXT /* Sub-category of the food group (e.g., citrus fruits, leafy greens) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("food_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nutrients" (
  "nutrient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each nutrient */,
  "nutrient_name" TEXT /* Name of the nutrient (e.g., energy, protein, fat) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the nutrient (e.g., kcal, g, mg) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the nutrient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("nutrient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "food_nutrients" (
  "food_nutrient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each food-nutrient pair */,
  "food_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food group */,
  "nutrient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the nutrient */,
  "quantity" REAL /* Quantity of the nutrient in the food group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("food_nutrient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_food_nutrients_food_id FOREIGN KEY ("food_id") REFERENCES food_groups ("food_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_food_nutrients_nutrient_id FOREIGN KEY ("nutrient_id") REFERENCES nutrients ("nutrient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "consumption_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "group_id" INTEGER /* ID of the population group */,
  "food_id" INTEGER /* ID of the food group */,
  "subjects" INTEGER /* Number of subjects in the study */,
  "consumers" INTEGER /* Number of consumers for the food group */,
  "mean_consumption" REAL /* Mean consumption value for the food group */,
  "sd_consumption" REAL /* Standard deviation of consumption for the food group */,
  "survey_year" INTEGER /* Year the survey was conducted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_consumption_data_food_id FOREIGN KEY ("food_id") REFERENCES food_groups ("food_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_consumption_data_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("group_id") REFERENCES population_groups ("group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_consumption_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publications" (
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publication */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the publication */,
  "authors" TEXT /* Authors of the publication */,
  "journal" TEXT /* Journal where the publication was published */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the publication was published */,
  "doi" TEXT /* DOI of the publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_publications" (
  "user_publication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-publication pair */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "publication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the publication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the publication (e.g., author, reviewer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_publication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_publications_publication_id FOREIGN KEY ("publication_id") REFERENCES publications ("publication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_publications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_controls" (
  "access_control_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "table_name" TEXT /* Name of the table */,
  "read_permission" INTEGER /* Whether the user has read permission for the table */,
  "write_permission" INTEGER /* Whether the user has write permission for the table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_control_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_controls_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
